text: So we always start the procedure with the pulmonary ligaments and the mediastinal dissection.
So after removing station nine, we go for the seven and we dissect the inferior pulmonary vein.
We identify the branch of V6 that will be stapled.
we continue the radical lymph node dissection with station 10 taking care of the artery thanks to the great dissection of the robot
We also go for the station 5 and 6, taking care of the nerves.
We move anteriorly with an easy fissure and we locate the branch of A6 with the help of 3D reconstruction.
Operative planning is a routine in our department for anatomical variants and margin.
Here we staple the only arterial branch for S6.
Then we inject ICG to demarcate the target segmentectomy.
We spot the intersegmental plane and we can check it with the preoperative reconstruction and the S-SYS in brown.
Here is a branch B6 with a carcinoid tumor inside.
We open the secondary carina to look at the tumor and remove it on block with margin.
We control with an endoscopic approach.
The monopolar scissors are very helpful to open the bronchus.
all the margins will be checked in frozen section we can see here macroscopical margin
So then we perform the intentional S6 segmentectomy and we stamp the parenchyma following the marks.
After that, the reconstruction is done with two continuous sutures of VLOC 3.0.
Note that the pulmonary artery is retracted laterally with the force arms of the robot to achieve a perfect exposure.
The dexterity of the robot of the platform is very helpful for this time.
At the end of the reconstruction, we tie the two V-locks and we control the anastomosis.
At the end, we obtain an excellent inflation of the remaining lung and we have a very good X-ray and a good outcome.
